judgmentZh: 同人于野，亨。利涉大川，利君子贞。
judgmentEn: Fellowship with Men in the open. Success. It furthers one to cross the great water. The perseverance of the superior man furthers.
imageZh: 天与火，同人；君子以类族辨物。
imageEn: Heaven together with fire: the image of Fellowship with Men. Thus the superior man organizes the clans and makes distinctions between things.
interpretationZh: 同人卦象征与人和同，天火同升，志同道合。在旷野中与人和同，光明正大，可成大事。
interpretationEn: Tong Ren symbolizes fellowship and harmony among people. Heaven and fire move together upward. Open fellowship in the wilderness leads to great achievements.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Fellowship with men at the gate. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在门口与人和同，刚开始便以开放之心待人，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship begins at the gate. An open heart from the start brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九“同人于门，无咎”描述了在门口与人和同的情景。“门”是家与外界的交界处，象征团结的起点。初九刚刚开始践行同人之道，从自家门口开始与人交往——这是最自然、最没有私心的状态。“无咎”说明只要以开放的心态迎接来访者，不设门槛、不分亲疏，就不会有过失。这一爻告诉我们：团结从身边开始，从打开家门的那一刻开始。不要等到需要帮助时才想起联络他人，平时就应该保持开放和友善的态度。
lines.detailEn: The first nine, 'Fellowship with men at the gate. No blame,' describes meeting others at the doorway. The 'gate' is the boundary between home and the outside world, symbolizing the starting point of fellowship. The first nine has just begun practicing the way of Tong Ren, starting to engage with others right at the doorstep—the most natural and selfless state. 'No blame' indicates that as long as one greets visitors with an open heart, without setting thresholds or distinguishing between close and distant, there will be no fault. This line teaches that fellowship begins nearby, from the moment you open your door. Don't wait until you need help to reach out to others—maintain an open and friendly attitude at all times.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Fellowship with men in the clan. Humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 只在宗族内部和同，心胸狭隘，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship limited to the clan is narrow-minded and leads to humiliation.
lines.detailZh: 六二“同人于宗，吝”是同人卦中唯一的阴爻，也是唯一带有负面评价的爻。“宗”指宗族、宗派，“吝”意为困窘、羞吝。六二只在自己的宗族内部和同，不愿向外扩展——这是一种狭隘的团结。六二与九五正应，本应与九五和同，却被宗族的小圈子所局限。这一爻严厉批评了“小圈子”文化：只与自己人抱团，排斥外人，看似团结实则是最大的分裂。在现代社会中，这相当于只在同乡会、校友圈中活动，错失了更广阔的合作机会。
lines.detailEn: The second six, 'Fellowship with men in the clan. Humiliation,' is Tong Ren's only yin line and the only line with a negative assessment. 'Clan' refers to one's own tribe or faction; 'humiliation' means embarrassment and limitation. The second six only associates within its own clan, unwilling to extend outward—this is narrow fellowship. Though the second six properly corresponds with the fifth nine and should unite with it, it remains confined by its small circle. This line sharply criticizes 'clique culture': bonding only with insiders while excluding outsiders may appear united but is actually the greatest form of division. In modern society, this is like only networking within alumni groups or hometown associations, missing far broader opportunities for collaboration.

lines.textZh: 九三：伏戎于莽，升其高陵，三岁不兴。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: He hides weapons in the thicket; he climbs the high hill in front of it. For three years he does not rise.
lines.interpretationZh: 在草丛中埋伏兵力，登上高丘观望，三年不能兴起，心怀猜忌难成事。
lines.interpretationEn: Hiding weapons in the thicket and climbing the hill to spy. For three years nothing is accomplished. Suspicion prevents success.
lines.detailZh: 九三“伏戎于莽，升其高陵，三岁不兴”描绘了一幅充满猜忌的画面。“伏戎于莽”——在草丛中埋伏军队；“升其高陵”——登上高丘窥探；“三岁不兴”——三年都无法兴起。九三想要与六二和同，但因为六二与九五正应而心生嫉妒，于是采取了阴谋手段——埋伏兵力、暗中窥探。然而这种基于猜忌和阴谋的“团结”注定失败，三年都无法成事。这一爻警示我们：真正的团结建立在信任之上，而非阴谋之上。心怀猜忌、暗中算计的人，永远无法赢得他人的真心。
lines.detailEn: The third nine, 'He hides weapons in the thicket; he climbs the high hill in front of it. For three years he does not rise,' paints a picture filled with suspicion. 'Hiding weapons in the thicket'—concealing troops in ambush; 'climbing the high hill'—ascending to spy from above; 'for three years he does not rise'—three years pass without success. The third nine desires fellowship with the second six, but jealous that the second six properly corresponds with the fifth nine, it resorts to scheming—laying ambushes and secretly spying. Yet this kind of 'unity' based on suspicion and conspiracy is doomed to fail, achieving nothing for three years. This line warns that genuine fellowship is built on trust, not intrigue. Those who harbor suspicion and scheme in secret can never win others' true hearts.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He climbs up on his wall; he cannot attack. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 登上城墙却不进攻，知难而退，反而吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Climbing the wall but refraining from attack. Restraint brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九四“乘其墉，弗克攻，吉”描述了一个关键的转折。“乘其墉”——登上了城墙，准备进攻；“弗克攻”——却没有发起攻击。九四本来想要强行争夺六二，已经登上城墙准备动手，但在最后关头醒悟过来，放弃了攻击。“吉”——正因为这个明智的放弃，反而获得了吉利。这一爻揭示了同人卦的重要智慧：知道什么时候该放手比知道什么时候该争取更重要。强行争夺来的关系不是真正的团结，主动放弃不属于自己的东西，反而能赢得更多的尊重和机会。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine, 'He climbs up on his wall; he cannot attack. Good fortune,' describes a crucial turning point. 'Climbing the wall'—mounting the rampart, ready to attack; 'cannot attack'—yet refraining from launching the assault. The fourth nine originally intended to forcibly compete for the second six's allegiance, having already scaled the wall to strike, but at the last moment came to its senses and abandoned the attack. 'Good fortune'—precisely because of this wise restraint, good fortune results. This line reveals an important wisdom of Tong Ren: knowing when to let go is more important than knowing when to fight. Relationships seized by force are not genuine fellowship; voluntarily releasing what doesn't belong to you actually earns greater respect and opens more opportunities.

lines.textZh: 九五：同人，先号啕而后笑，大师克相遇。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Men bound in fellowship first weep and lament, but afterward they laugh. After great struggles they succeed in meeting.
lines.interpretationZh: 同心之人先经历悲泣，后终欢笑，经过艰难斗争终于相聚。
lines.interpretationEn: Those in fellowship first weep, then laugh. After great struggles they finally meet.
lines.detailZh: 九五“同人，先号啕而后笑，大师克相遇”是同人卦最感人的一爻。“先号啕而后笑”——先是痛哭流涕，后来破涕为笑，描绘了真正的知己经历重重阻碍后终于相聚的动人场景。“大师克相遇”意为经过大规模的艰难斗争，终于得以相见。九五与六二正应，本应和同，但中间隔着九三、九四两个阳爻的阻挡，必须经过艰苦的努力才能突破障碍。这一爻告诉我们：真正的知己和真正的团结，往往需要经历考验和磨难。轻易得到的友谊未必深厚，历经风雨的情谊才最珍贵。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine, 'Men bound in fellowship first weep and lament, but afterward they laugh. After great struggles they succeed in meeting,' is Tong Ren's most moving line. 'First weeping, then laughing'—first tears of sorrow, then tears of joy—depicts the touching scene of true kindred spirits finally reuniting after overcoming tremendous obstacles. 'After great struggles they succeed in meeting' means that through large-scale, arduous effort, they finally come together. The fifth nine properly corresponds with the second six and should be in fellowship, but the third and fourth yang lines stand between them as barriers that must be broken through with great effort. This line teaches that true fellowship and genuine unity often require enduring trials and tribulations. Easily won friendships may lack depth; bonds forged through storms are the most precious.

lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Fellowship with men in the meadow. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 在郊外与人和同，虽未达到旷野般的广阔，但也无悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Fellowship in the meadow. Though not the widest circle, there is no remorse.
lines.detailZh: 上九“同人于郊，无悔”描述了在郊外与人和同的状态。“郊”比“野”范围小，比“宗”范围大，是一个中间地带。上九处于同人卦的最高位置，已经超越了争斗和阻碍，在城郊与人和平相处。虽然没有达到“同人于野”那样最广泛的团结，但也没有“同人于宗”那样的狭隘，所以“无悔”。这一爻告诉我们：团结不必追求完美，能在自己的能力范围内实现尽可能广泛的和同，就已经足够好了。不必为无法团结所有人而遗憾，做到问心无愧即可。
lines.detailEn: The top nine, 'Fellowship with men in the meadow. No remorse,' describes fellowship in the outskirts. The 'meadow' is smaller in scope than the 'open wilderness' but broader than the 'clan'—a middle ground. The top nine occupies Tong Ren's highest position, having transcended conflict and obstruction, peacefully associating with others in the suburban fields. Though it hasn't achieved the broadest fellowship of 'the open,' neither has it fallen into the narrowness of 'the clan,' hence 'no remorse.' This line teaches that fellowship need not be perfect—achieving the broadest possible unity within one's capacity is already good enough. Don't regret being unable to unite everyone; being at peace with your conscience is sufficient.

overviewZh: 同人卦是《周易》第十三卦，由上乾（天）下离（火）组成，象征火焰向上与天相合，志同道合之人聚集在一起。“同人”即与人和同、与人团结之意。卦辞“同人于野”强调在旷野中与人和同——不是在狭小的宗族或派系内部结党，而是在广阔的天地间以公心团结众人。火性炎上，与天同行，象征光明正大的联合。同人卦的核心精神是“大同”——超越血缘、地域、派系的狭隘界限，以共同的理想和正义为纽带，实现最广泛的团结。这是一种开放、包容、光明磊落的合作精神。
overviewEn: Tong Ren (Fellowship with Men) is the 13th hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Qian (heaven) above and Li (fire) below, symbolizing flames rising upward to meet heaven—like-minded people gathering together in common purpose. 'Tong Ren' means fellowship, unity, and harmony among people. The judgment 'Fellowship with men in the open' emphasizes uniting with others in the vast wilderness—not forming narrow cliques within clan or faction, but bringing people together with public-spirited purpose across the broad expanse of heaven and earth. Fire by nature rises upward, moving in concert with heaven, symbolizing an open and honorable alliance. The core spirit of Tong Ren is 'great unity'—transcending the narrow boundaries of blood, territory, and faction, using shared ideals and justice as the bond to achieve the broadest possible solidarity. This is a spirit of open, inclusive, and upright cooperation.
judgmentDetailZh: 同人卦辞“同人于野，亨。利涉大川，利君子贞”层层递进。“同人于野”是最高境界的团结——“野”为旷野，象征最广阔的空间，意味着不分亲疏远近、不论门第出身，在最开放的场域中与人和同。“亨”表示这种广泛的团结必然通达顺利。“利涉大川”意为有利于渡过大河——当志同道合的人团结在一起，就有力量克服最大的困难。“利君子贞”强调这种团结必须建立在正道之上，是君子之间基于共同理想的联合，而非小人之间基于私利的勾结。《彖传》释曰：“文明以健，中正而应，君子正也。唯君子为能通天下之志。”只有光明正大、中正不偏的君子，才能真正沟通天下人的心志，实现大同。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Fellowship with men in the open. Success. It furthers one to cross the great water. The perseverance of the superior man furthers' builds progressively. 'Fellowship in the open' represents the highest level of unity—'the open' signifies the vast wilderness, meaning unity without regard to closeness or distance, birth or status, in the most expansive possible arena. 'Success' indicates that such broad fellowship inevitably leads to smooth progress. 'It furthers one to cross the great water' means that when like-minded people unite, they possess the strength to overcome the greatest obstacles. 'The perseverance of the superior man furthers' emphasizes that this unity must be founded on righteous principles—an alliance of noble persons based on shared ideals, not a conspiracy of petty people based on private gain. The Commentary states: 'Civilized yet strong, centered and correct with proper correspondence—this is the way of the superior man. Only the superior man can unite the wills of all under heaven.'
imageDetailZh: 象辞“天与火，同人。君子以类族辨物”描绘了天与火同行的景象。火焰向上燃烧，与天的方向一致，象征志向相同的人自然走到一起。“类族辨物”意为按照类别区分族群和事物——这看似与“大同”矛盾，实则揭示了真正团结的前提：先要清楚地认识事物的异同，才能在尊重差异的基础上求同存异。真正的“同”不是消灭差异的一致，而是在承认差异的基础上找到共同点。君子“类族辨物”的智慧在于：既能看到人与人之间的不同，又能找到超越不同的共同理想。这与现代社会“多元一体”的理念完全吻合——尊重多样性，同时追求共同价值。
imageDetailEn: The Image states 'Heaven together with fire: the image of Fellowship with Men. Thus the superior man organizes the clans and makes distinctions between things.' Fire burns upward in the same direction as heaven, symbolizing people with aligned aspirations naturally coming together. 'Organizing clans and distinguishing things' means categorizing groups and matters according to their nature—this seemingly contradicts 'great unity' but actually reveals the prerequisite for genuine fellowship: one must first clearly understand similarities and differences before seeking common ground while respecting diversity. True 'fellowship' is not uniformity that eliminates differences but finding commonality while acknowledging distinctions. The wisdom of 'organizing and distinguishing' lies in the ability to see differences between people while simultaneously discovering shared ideals that transcend those differences. This perfectly aligns with the modern concept of 'unity in diversity'—respecting multiplicity while pursuing common values.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】同人卦是团队合作最吉利的卦象之一。“同人于野”提示要打破部门壁垒、消除派系隔阂，以共同的目标凝聚团队。创业者应寻找志同道合的合伙人，而非仅凭利益关系结合。跨部门协作、行业联盟、开源社区等都是同人精神的现代体现。关键是“利君子贞”——合作必须建立在共同的价值观之上。【感情】同人卦在感情中代表志趣相投、心灵相通。最好的伴侣不是外在条件的匹配，而是内在理想的契合。“先号啕而后笑”提示真正的知己可能需要经历考验才能相遇。【财运】同人卦利于合伙经营、众筹投资等集体理财方式。“利涉大川”暗示团结的力量可以克服单打独斗无法解决的财务困难。但要注意选择合作伙伴的品格。【健康】同人卦对应心脏和眼睛（离为火为目），提醒注意心血管健康和用眼卫生。社交活动有助于心理健康，但要避免过度应酬。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Tong Ren is one of the most auspicious hexagrams for teamwork. 'Fellowship in the open' advises breaking down departmental barriers and eliminating factional divisions, uniting teams around shared goals. Entrepreneurs should seek partners who share their vision, not merely those connected by profit. Cross-departmental collaboration, industry alliances, and open-source communities are modern expressions of the Tong Ren spirit. The key is 'the perseverance of the superior man'—cooperation must be built on shared values.【Love】In relationships, Tong Ren represents shared interests and spiritual connection. The best partner is not one who matches external conditions but one whose inner ideals align with yours. 'First weeping, then laughing' suggests that true soulmates may need to endure trials before finding each other.【Wealth】Tong Ren favors partnerships, crowdfunding, and collective financial ventures. 'Crossing the great water' suggests that united strength can overcome financial difficulties impossible to solve alone. But choose partners of good character.【Health】Tong Ren corresponds to the heart and eyes (Li represents fire and vision). Pay attention to cardiovascular health and eye care. Social activities benefit mental health, but avoid excessive socializing.
historicalStoryZh: 同人卦最著名的历史典故是刘备“桃园三结义”的故事。东汉末年，刘备、关羽、张飞三人虽出身不同——刘备织席贩履、关羽亡命天涯、张飞屠猪卖酒——却因共同的匡扶汉室之志而结为兄弟。这正是“同人于野”的精神：超越出身和地位的差异，以共同的理想为纽带。三人此后历经艰辛，“先号啕而后笑”，最终建立蜀汉政权。另一个典故是管仲与鲍叔牙的友谊。鲍叔牙深知管仲之才，不计个人得失向齐桓公举荐管仲为相，成就了齐国的霸业。这种超越私利的“同人”精神，正是同人卦“利君子贞”的最佳写照。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous historical story of Tong Ren is the 'Oath of the Peach Garden' by Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei. At the end of the Eastern Han dynasty, these three men came from vastly different backgrounds—Liu Bei wove mats and sold sandals, Guan Yu was a fugitive, and Zhang Fei was a butcher and wine seller—yet they swore brotherhood based on their shared aspiration to restore the Han dynasty. This perfectly embodies 'fellowship in the open': transcending differences in birth and status, united by common ideals. The three endured tremendous hardships together—'first weeping, then laughing'—ultimately establishing the Shu Han kingdom. Another story involves the friendship between Guan Zhong and Bao Shuya. Bao Shuya, recognizing Guan Zhong's extraordinary talent, selflessly recommended him as prime minister to Duke Huan of Qi, setting aside personal gain to create Qi's hegemony. This spirit of fellowship beyond self-interest perfectly illustrates Tong Ren's principle that 'the perseverance of the superior man furthers.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 同人卦的错卦为师卦（第7卦），同人为和平的团结，师为军事的统率，一文一武相对应；综卦为大有卦（第14卦），先团结众人（同人），后获得大丰收（大有）。互卦为姤卦（第44卦），暗示团结中潜藏的分歧与相遇的机缘。

judgmentEn: Possession in Great Measure. Supreme success.
imageZh: 火在天上，大有；君子以遏恶扬善，顺天休命。
imageEn: Fire in heaven above: the image of Possession in Great Measure. Thus the superior man curbs evil and furthers good, and thereby obeys the benevolent will of heaven.
interpretationZh: 大有卦象征大丰收，火在天上，光明普照。柔居尊位，上下应之，万物亨通。
interpretationEn: Da You symbolizes great possession and abundance. Fire shines high in heaven, illuminating all. A yielding line in the place of honor receives support from all, bringing supreme success.

lines.textZh: 初九：无交害，匪咎，艰则无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: No relationship with what is harmful; there is no blame in this. If one remains conscious of difficulty, one remains without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不与有害之事交往，本身无过。保持警惕艰苦之心，则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Avoid harmful associations. Remaining aware of difficulties keeps one free of blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九“无交害，匪咎，艰则无咎”描述了大有初始阶段的谨慎态度。“无交害”意为不与有害之事交往，保持清白；“匪咎”意为本身没有过失；“艰则无咎”意为保持艰苦奋斗的心态就不会有过失。初九处于大有卦最下方，虽然整体卦象大吉，但初始阶段不可掉以轻心。刚刚开始拥有财富和成就时，最容易被诱惑和陷阱所害。这一爻告诉我们：越是在丰收的开端，越要保持警惕和艰苦朴素的作风。不与不良势力交往，不因小利而失大节，才能为长久的“大有”奠定基础。
lines.detailEn: The first nine, 'No relationship with what is harmful; there is no blame in this. If one remains conscious of difficulty, one remains without blame,' describes the cautious attitude at the beginning of great possession. 'No relationship with what is harmful' means avoiding harmful associations and maintaining integrity; 'no blame' means being inherently faultless; 'conscious of difficulty' means maintaining a mindset of diligent effort to avoid blame. The first nine sits at the bottom of Da You; though the overall hexagram is highly auspicious, one must not be careless at the start. When first acquiring wealth and achievement, one is most vulnerable to temptation and traps. This line teaches that the very beginning of abundance demands the greatest vigilance and simplicity.

lines.textZh: 九二：大车以载，有攸往，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A big wagon for loading. One may undertake something. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大车装载货物，有所前往，无过失。才能充足，堪当重任。
lines.interpretationEn: A great wagon for loading. One may set forth. Capable of bearing heavy responsibility.
lines.detailZh: 九二“大车以载，有攸往，无咎”以大车装载货物为喻，描绘了才能充足、堪当重任的形象。“大车”象征宽大的容量和承载力，九二居中得位，刚健而不偏激，如同一辆坚固的大车，能够承载大量的货物而稳步前行。“有攸往”意为有所前往——有了充足的准备和能力，可以放心地去做大事。这一爻告诉我们：在大有的时代，最重要的是具备承载丰盛的能力。财富和机会来了，你是否有足够的“大车”来装载？能力、品德、团队、制度，都是承载丰盛的“大车”。
lines.detailEn: The second nine, 'A big wagon for loading. One may undertake something. No blame,' uses the image of a great wagon carrying goods to depict someone with ample talent capable of bearing heavy responsibility. The 'big wagon' symbolizes vast capacity and carrying power. The second nine is centered and correctly positioned, strong yet balanced, like a sturdy wagon that can carry heavy loads while moving steadily forward. 'One may undertake something' means that with sufficient preparation and ability, one can confidently pursue great endeavors. This line teaches that in times of great possession, the most important thing is having the capacity to handle abundance.

lines.textZh: 九三：公用亨于天子，小人弗克。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven. A petty man cannot do this.
lines.interpretationZh: 公侯将财富献给天子，小人做不到如此大度。
lines.interpretationEn: A prince offers his wealth to the Son of Heaven. A petty man is incapable of such generosity.
lines.detailZh: 九三“公用亨于天子，小人弗克”描述了将财富献给天子的高尚行为。“公”指公侯，“亨于天子”意为将丰盛的成果进献给天子。九三处于下卦之顶，已经积累了相当的财富和功绩，此时选择将成果奉献给更高的事业——这是大有卦“遏恶扬善”精神的具体体现。“小人弗克”意为小人做不到这一点——小人只会独占财富，不懂得分享和奉献。这一爻揭示了大有卦的核心价值观：真正的大有不是占有，而是奉献。能够将个人的成就转化为公共的福祉，才是最高境界的“大有”。
lines.detailEn: The third nine, 'A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven. A petty man cannot do this,' describes the noble act of presenting wealth to the sovereign. The third nine sits at the top of the lower trigram, having accumulated considerable wealth and merit, and now chooses to dedicate these achievements to a higher cause—this is the concrete expression of Da You's spirit of 'curbing evil and furthering good.' 'A petty man cannot do this' means small-minded people are incapable of such generosity—they can only hoard wealth, unable to share or contribute. This line reveals Da You's core value: true great possession is not about holding but about giving.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不炫耀自己的丰盛，保持谦逊，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: He does not flaunt his abundance. Modesty brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: 九四“匪其彭，无咎”中“彭”意为盛大、丰满。“匪其彭”意为不炫耀自己的丰盛——虽然拥有很多，但不张扬、不显摆。九四处于上卦之始，紧邻六五尊位，位高权重却选择低调内敛。这一爻揭示了大有时期最重要的处世智慧：越是拥有得多，越要保持谦逊低调。炫富招祸、骄奢致败，这是千古不变的教训。在现代社会中，这相当于成功人士保持低调作风，不在社交媒体上炫耀财富，不以物质攀比为乐。真正的富有是内心的充实，而非外在的炫耀。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine, 'He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor. No blame'—here the meaning is not flaunting one's prosperity—possessing much yet remaining unassuming and modest. The fourth nine sits at the beginning of the upper trigram, adjacent to the honored fifth position, holding high rank and great power yet choosing restraint and discretion. This line reveals the most important wisdom for times of great possession: the more one has, the more one must maintain humble modesty. Flaunting wealth invites disaster; arrogance and excess lead to ruin—this is an eternal lesson. True richness is inner fulfillment, not outward display.

lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: He whose truth is accessible, yet dignified, has good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信与人交往，又不失威严，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: One whose sincerity is approachable yet dignified has good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五“厥孚交如，威如，吉”是大有卦的核心爻位，也是全卦唯一的阴爻。“厥孚交如”意为以诚信与人交往，“威如”意为同时保持威严。六五以柔居尊位，不靠强权而靠诚信来凝聚人心，同时又不失领导者的威严和尊严。这是最理想的领导风格——既亲和又有威信，既谦逊又有气度。“吉”字说明这种领导方式必然带来吉祥。在现代管理中，这相当于“有温度的领导力”——让下属感到被信任和尊重，同时又对领导者心存敬畏。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, 'He whose truth is accessible, yet dignified, has good fortune,' is Da You's core line and the hexagram's only yin line. 'Truth that is accessible' means engaging others with sincerity; 'yet dignified' means simultaneously maintaining authority and gravitas. The fifth six occupies the honored position with yielding grace, uniting hearts not through force but through sincerity, while never losing a leader's dignity and presence. This is the ideal leadership style—approachable yet authoritative, humble yet commanding. 'Good fortune' confirms that this leadership approach inevitably brings auspicious results.

lines.textZh: 上九：自天祐之，吉无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He is blessed by heaven. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 得到上天的庇佑，吉利，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Blessed by heaven. Good fortune in everything undertaken.
lines.detailZh: 上九“自天祐之，吉无不利”是大有卦最辉煌的结局，也是整部《周易》中最吉利的爻辞之一。“自天祐之”意为得到上天的庇佑——这不是迷信，而是因果法则的体现。孔子在《系辞传》中解释：“祐者，助也。天之所助者，顺也；人之所助者，信也。履信思乎顺，又以尚贤也，是以自天祐之，吉无不利也。”上天帮助顺应天道的人，人们帮助诚信的人。当一个人既顺应天道又诚信待人，还能尊重贤能，那么天人共助，自然吉无不利。这一爻告诉我们：最大的好运不是偶然的幸运，而是长期积累善行和德行的必然结果。
lines.detailEn: The top nine, 'He is blessed by heaven. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further,' is Da You's most glorious conclusion and one of the most auspicious line texts in the entire I Ching. 'Blessed by heaven' means receiving heaven's protection—this is not superstition but the manifestation of the law of cause and effect. Confucius explained in the Xici Commentary: 'To bless means to help. Heaven helps those who are devoted; people help those who are sincere. One who walks in sincerity and thinks of devotion, and furthermore honors the worthy—therefore heaven blesses him, bringing good fortune and nothing unfurthered.' This line teaches that the greatest good fortune is not random luck but the inevitable result of long-accumulated good deeds and virtue.

overviewZh: 大有卦是《周易》第十四卦，由上离（火）下乾（天）组成，象征火在天上，光明普照万物。离为火、为日，高悬天际，照耀四方，万物无不沐浴其光辉——这就是“大有”的意象：拥有广大、丰盛富足。大有卦与同人卦互为综卦，同人是团结众人，大有则是团结之后获得的丰硕成果。卦中六五以一阴居尊位，柔顺中正，上下五个阳爻皆来归附，如同明君在位、群贤毕至。大有卦的核心精神是“以德聚财”——真正的大有不是靠掠夺和独占，而是以光明的德行感召天下，让财富和人才自然汇聚。
overviewEn: Da You (Possession in Great Measure) is the 14th hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Li (fire) above and Qian (heaven) below, symbolizing fire blazing high in the sky, illuminating all things beneath. Li as fire and sun hangs in the heavens, shining in all directions, bathing everything in its radiance—this is the image of 'great possession': vast holdings and abundant prosperity. Da You and Tong Ren are inverse hexagrams; Tong Ren unites people, while Da You represents the abundant harvest that follows unity. The single yin line at the fifth position occupies the place of honor with yielding centrality, and all five yang lines below and above rally to it—like a wise sovereign on the throne with all worthy ministers gathered around. The core spirit of Da You is 'gathering wealth through virtue'—true great possession comes not from plunder and monopoly but from attracting the world's resources and talents through luminous moral character.
judgmentDetailZh: 大有卦辞“大有：元亨”仅四字，却气象万千。“元亨”即大通、至亨，是最高等级的通达顺利。《彖传》释曰：“大有，柔得尊位大中，而上下应之，曰大有。其德刚健而文明，应乎天而时行，是以元亨。”六五阴爻居于尊位，得中得正，以柔居刚，上下五个阳爻皆与之呼应——这是“以柔驭刚”的最高境界。“刚健而文明”指内在刚健（乾）而外在光明（离），既有实力又有德行。“应乎天而时行”意为顺应天道、把握时机而行动。大有卦告诉我们：最大的成功不是靠强力征服，而是靠德行感召。当一个人的品德足够光明，天下的资源和人才自然会向他汇聚。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Possession in Great Measure. Supreme success' contains only four characters yet conveys boundless grandeur. 'Supreme success' represents the highest level of smooth achievement. The Commentary explains: 'In Great Possession, the yielding obtains the honored position, great and central, and those above and below respond to it—this is called Great Possession. Its virtue is strong and bright, responsive to heaven and timely in action, hence supreme success.' The yin line at the fifth position occupies the place of honor, centered and correct, softness dwelling in firmness, with all five yang lines responding—this is the supreme art of 'governing strength through gentleness.' 'Strong and bright' means inwardly powerful (Qian) yet outwardly luminous (Li), possessing both capability and virtue. 'Responsive to heaven and timely in action' means following heaven's way and acting at the right moment. Da You teaches that the greatest success comes not from forceful conquest but from moral inspiration. When a person's character shines brightly enough, the world's resources and talents naturally gravitate toward them.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“火在天上，大有。君子以遏恶扬善，顺天休命”描绘了太阳高悬天际的壮丽景象。火在天上，光明无所不照，善恶无所遁形——这就是大有卦给君子的启示。“遏恶扬善”意为抑制邪恶、弘扬善良，这是拥有大权大势者的首要责任。“顺天休命”中“休”意为美善，“顺天休命”即顺应天道的美善旨意。拥有巨大财富和权力的人，应当像太阳一样公正无私地照耀万物——不是用权力谋取私利，而是用权力遏制邪恶、弘扬正义。这与现代企业社会责任的理念高度一致：财富越大，责任越大。真正的“大有”不仅是物质的丰盛，更是精神的光明。
imageDetailEn: The Image states 'Fire in heaven above: the image of Possession in Great Measure. Thus the superior man curbs evil and furthers good, and thereby obeys the benevolent will of heaven.' This depicts the magnificent scene of the sun blazing high in the sky. Fire in heaven illuminates everything without exception, leaving no place for evil to hide—this is Da You's instruction to the noble person. 'Curbing evil and furthering good' means suppressing wickedness and promoting virtue, the primary responsibility of those who hold great power and influence. 'Obeying the benevolent will of heaven' means following heaven's gracious intent. Those who possess great wealth and power should shine like the sun—impartially illuminating all things, using power not for private gain but to suppress evil and promote justice. This aligns remarkably with modern concepts of corporate social responsibility: the greater the wealth, the greater the responsibility. True 'great possession' is not merely material abundance but spiritual luminosity.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】大有卦是事业丰收的大吉之卦。“火在天上”象征事业如日中天，声望远播。此时适合扩大规模、开拓市场、招揽人才。但要牢记“遏恶扬善”——成功之后更要坚守正道，不可因富贵而骄奢淫逸。领导者应以德服人，让团队成员都能分享成功的果实。【感情】大有卦在感情中代表丰盈美满的状态。关系中充满信任和温暖，双方都能感受到被珍视。六五“厥孚交如，威如”提示在亲密中保持适度的尊重和距离，感情才能长久。【财运】大有卦是财运最旺的卦象之一，预示丰厚的收入和回报。但“匪其彭，无咎”提醒不可炫富，“公用亨于天子”鼓励将财富用于公益事业。越是富有，越要懂得回馈社会。【健康】大有卦对应心脏和头部（离为火为心，乾为首），提醒注意心脑血管健康。事业繁忙时更要注意休息，避免过度劳累导致心火上炎。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Da You is a supremely auspicious hexagram for career harvest. 'Fire in heaven' symbolizes a career at its zenith with far-reaching reputation. This is the time to expand scale, develop markets, and recruit talent. But remember 'curbing evil and furthering good'—after success, one must adhere even more firmly to righteous principles and avoid arrogance and excess. Leaders should inspire through virtue and share the fruits of success with their teams.【Love】In relationships, Da You represents a state of abundance and fulfillment. The relationship is filled with trust and warmth, with both partners feeling cherished. The fifth six's 'sincerity that is accessible yet dignified' advises maintaining appropriate respect and space within intimacy for lasting love.【Wealth】Da You is one of the most financially auspicious hexagrams, portending generous income and returns. Yet 'he does not flaunt his abundance' warns against showing off wealth, and 'a prince offers to the Son of Heaven' encourages using wealth for public benefit. The wealthier one becomes, the more important it is to give back to society.【Health】Da You corresponds to the heart and head (Li represents fire and heart; Qian represents the head). Pay attention to cardiovascular and cerebrovascular health. During busy career periods, rest is essential—avoid overwork that inflames the heart fire.
historicalStoryZh: 大有卦最著名的历史典故是唐太宗李世民开创“贞观之治”的故事。李世民即位后，以六五“厥孚交如，威如”的精神治国——既以诚信与群臣交往，又不失天子威严。他广纳贤才，房玄龄、杜如晦、魏征等名臣辅佐左右，如同大有卦五阳归附一阴的格局。贞观年间国力强盛、四夷来朝、百姓富足，正是“大有：元亨”的盛世写照。李世民深谙“遏恶扬善”之道，设立谏官制度，鼓励直言进谏，以光明正大的治理赢得了天下归心。另一个典故是范蠡功成身退后经商致富，三次散尽家财又三次重新积累，体现了“大有”而不执着于“有”的超然智慧。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous historical story of Da You is Emperor Taizong of Tang and his creation of the 'Reign of Zhenguan.' After ascending the throne, Li Shimin governed with the spirit of the fifth six—'sincerity that is accessible yet dignified'—engaging ministers with genuine trust while maintaining imperial authority. He recruited brilliant talents: Fang Xuanling, Du Ruhui, Wei Zheng, and other renowned ministers served at his side, mirroring Da You's pattern of five yang lines rallying around one yin. During the Zhenguan era, national power flourished, foreign nations paid tribute, and the people prospered—a perfect embodiment of 'Great Possession: Supreme Success.' Li Shimin mastered the art of 'curbing evil and furthering good,' establishing the remonstrance system and encouraging frank counsel, winning the hearts of all through luminous governance. Another story involves Fan Li, who after helping King Goujian achieve victory, retired to become a merchant, amassing and dispersing great fortunes three times over—embodying the transcendent wisdom of possessing greatly without clinging to possession.
relatedHexagramsNote: 大有卦的错卦为比卦（第8卦），大有为众人归附于明君，比为众人亲附于领袖，一以德聚，一以情聚；综卦为同人卦（第13卦），先团结众人（同人），后获得丰收（大有）。互卦为夬卦（第43卦），暗示丰盛之中需要果断决策以防止阴邪侵蚀。

judgmentEn: Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through.
imageZh: 地中有山，谦；君子以裒多益寡，称物平施。
imageEn: Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. Thus the superior man reduces that which is too much, and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal.
interpretationZh: 谦卦象征谦逊，山在地中，高而不显。谦虚者亨通，君子以谦德善终。
interpretationEn: Qian symbolizes modesty. A mountain hidden within the earth — great yet unassuming. The modest person prospers and the superior man achieves a good end.

lines.textZh: 初六：谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: A superior man modest about his modesty may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦而又谦的君子，可以渡过大河，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man modest about his modesty. He may cross the great water. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 初六“谦谦君子，用涉大川，吉”描绘了谦虚到极致的君子形象。双重的“谦”字强调谦虚之上更加谦虚，这样的人即使面对“涉大川”般的重大挑战也能逢凶化吉。因为谦虚的人不会轻敌冒进，会充分准备、虚心请教，反而比自负的人更容易成功。在现代语境中，这相当于面对重大项目时保持敬畏心，充分调研、广泛咨询，成功概率反而最高。
lines.detailEn: The first six depicts the image of an extremely modest person. The doubled 'modest' emphasizes humility upon humility—such a person can safely cross even great rivers (face major challenges). Because humble people never underestimate difficulties, they prepare thoroughly and seek advice widely, actually succeeding more easily than the overconfident.

lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德之名远播，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二“鸣谦，贞吉”中的“鸣”意为声名远播——谦虚的美德已经被众人所知和赞颂。这不是刻意宣传，而是真诚的谦虚自然散发出的感染力。六二居中得位，是谦德最自然、最纯粹的表达。当一个人的谦虚发自内心而非做作，周围的人自然会感受到并给予尊重。这种“不求名而名自来”的境界，正是谦卦的精髓。
lines.detailEn: The second six—'Modesty that comes to expression. Perseverance brings good fortune'—describes modesty so genuine that its reputation naturally spreads. This is not deliberate publicity but the infectious power of sincere humility. When a person's modesty comes from the heart rather than affectation, others naturally sense it and respond with respect.

lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 勤劳而谦逊，君子有好的结局，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A superior man with merit yet modest carries things through. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九三是谦卦唯一的阳爻，“劳谦，君子有终，吉”描述了一位功劳卓著却依然谦虚的君子。“劳”指辛勤付出和显著功绩，“谦”指功成不居。这是谦卦最难能可贵的境界——有功劳的人保持谦虚远比无功之人谦虚更难。正因如此，“君子有终”——这样的人必定能善始善终，获得最好的结局。
lines.detailEn: The third nine is Kun's only yang line—'A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion. Good fortune.' This describes someone with outstanding achievements who remains humble. Having merit yet staying modest is far harder than being humble without accomplishments—precisely why such a person 'carries things to conclusion' with the best possible outcome.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Nothing that would not further modesty in movement.
lines.interpretationZh: 无往不利，发挥谦逊之德。
lines.interpretationEn: Nothing that would not further. Modesty expressed in action.
lines.detailZh: 六四“无不利，撝谦”中“撝”意为发挥、施展。此爻说的是在一切行动中都发挥谦虚的品德，没有什么不利的。六四处于上卦之始，接近权力中心，在这个位置上保持谦虚尤为重要——越是接近高位，越需要谦逊。这一爻告诉我们：谦虚不是某些场合的策略，而应该成为贯穿一切行为的品格。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six—'Nothing that would not further modesty in movement'—means exercising humility in all actions brings universal benefit. Near the center of power, maintaining modesty becomes especially crucial—the closer to high position, the greater the need for humility. This line teaches that modesty should not be a situational strategy but a character trait pervading all conduct.

lines.textZh: 六五：不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Not boasting of wealth before one's neighbor. It is favorable to attack with force. Nothing that would not further.
lines.interpretationZh: 不以财富在邻人面前炫耀，必要时可用武力征伐，无不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Not boasting of wealth before neighbors. When necessary, forceful action is favorable. Nothing unfurthered.
lines.detailZh: 六五“不富以其邻，利用侵伐，无不利”看似与谦虚矛盾，实则揭示了谦德的另一面。“不富以其邻”意为不以财富笼络邻国，而是以谦德感化；“利用侵伐”并非鼓励侵略，而是说当不得不采取强硬行动时，出于谦德的正义之举也是合理的。真正的谦虚不是无原则的退让，该坚持的原则必须坚持，该采取的行动必须果断。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six seems to contradict modesty but actually reveals its other dimension. 'Not rich through neighbors' means winning allegiance through virtue rather than wealth. 'It is favorable to attack' doesn't encourage aggression but indicates that when firm action is necessary, righteous action rooted in modesty is justified. True modesty is not unprincipled retreat—principles must be upheld and necessary action taken decisively.

lines.textZh: 上六：鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Modesty that comes to expression. It is favorable to set armies marching to chastise one's own city and one's country.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦德远闻，可以出兵征讨，整顿自己的城邑国家。
lines.interpretationEn: Modesty that finds expression. It is favorable to march armies to set one's own domain in order.
lines.detailZh: 上六“鸣谦，利用行师，征邑国”与六二的“鸣谦”呼应，但处于更高的位置。谦虚的声名已经广为传播，此时即使动用军队也是正当的——因为天下人都知道你的谦德，你的行动自然被视为正义之举。这一爻说明：长期积累的谦虚信誉是最强大的软实力。当你需要采取重大行动时，过去的谦德会成为最有力的支撑。
lines.detailEn: The top six echoes the second six's 'modesty that comes to expression' but from a higher position. When your reputation for modesty is widely known, even mobilizing forces is justified—because everyone recognizes your virtue, your actions are naturally seen as righteous. This line demonstrates that long-accumulated humble credibility is the most powerful soft power. When major action is needed, past modesty becomes your strongest support.

overviewZh: 谦卦是《周易》六十四卦中唯一六爻皆吉的卦，由上坤（地）下艮（山）组成，象征高山隐于地下。山本高大，却甘居地下，这就是谦虚的意象——有实力却不张扬，有才华却不自满。谦卦在六十四卦中地位特殊，《系辞》赞曰“谦，德之柄也”，意为谦虚是一切美德的把手，是修身处世的根本。无论处于何种境遇，谦虚都能带来吉祥，这在六十四卦中绝无仅有。
overviewEn: Qian (Modesty) is the only hexagram in the I Ching where all six lines are auspicious. Composed of Kun (earth) above and Gen (mountain) below, it symbolizes a great mountain hidden beneath the earth. The mountain is naturally tall and imposing, yet it willingly rests below the ground—this is the image of true modesty: possessing strength without display, having talent without arrogance. The Xici Commentary praises it: 'Modesty is the handle of virtue,' meaning humility is the foundation of all moral cultivation. Regardless of one's circumstances, modesty always brings good fortune—unique among all sixty-four hexagrams.
judgmentDetailZh: 谦卦卦辞“谦：亨，君子有终”简洁而深刻。“亨”表示通达顺利，“君子有终”意为有德行的人能善始善终。《彖传》进一步阐释：“天道亏盈而益谦，地道变盈而流谦，鬼神害盈而福谦，人道恶盈而好谦”——天道、地道、鬼神之道、人道，四者皆损满而益谦。这是宇宙间最普遍的法则：骄傲自满必遭损害，谦虚退让必得福报。月满则亏、水满则溢，唯有谦虚才能持盈保泰。孔子特别推崇此卦，认为谦德是君子最重要的品质。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment 'Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through' is concise yet profound. The Commentary elaborates: 'The way of heaven diminishes the full and augments the modest; the way of earth transforms the full and flows toward the modest; spirits and gods harm the full and bless the modest; the way of humans hates the full and loves the modest'—heaven, earth, spirits, and humanity all reduce excess and benefit modesty. This is the most universal law: pride inevitably brings harm, while humility always brings blessings. Confucius especially revered this hexagram, considering modesty the most essential quality of a noble person.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“地中有山，谦。君子以裒多益寡，称物平施”描绘了一幅意味深长的画面：大地之中蕴藏着高山，外表平坦却内含巍峨。“裒多益寡”意为减少多余的、补充不足的，“称物平施”意为衡量事物轻重而公平分配。这是谦卦给出的实践指南——谦虚不是空洞的姿态，而是要落实到行动中：削减自己过多的部分去帮助不足的人，公平公正地对待每一件事。这种“损有余补不足”的精神，正是社会和谐的基石。
imageDetailEn: The Image 'Within the earth, a mountain: the image of Modesty. The superior man reduces that which is too much and augments that which is too little. He weighs things and makes them equal' paints a profound picture: a great mountain contained within the earth, outwardly level yet inwardly towering. This is Modesty's practical guide—humility is not an empty gesture but must be realized through action: reducing one's excess to help those in need, treating everything with fairness and justice. This spirit of 'diminishing surplus to supplement deficiency' is the cornerstone of social harmony.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】谦卦提示在职场中保持谦逊低调是最稳妥的策略。功劳不必急于邀赏，能力不必处处炫耀。“地中有山”的智慧告诉我们：真正有实力的人不需要自我标榜，实力自然会被看见。领导者更应以谦德服人，而非以权势压人。【感情】谦卦在感情中代表真诚和尊重。放下自我中心，认真倾听对方的需求，不在关系中争高低。谦虚的态度能化解大部分矛盾，让关系更加和谐长久。【财运】谦卦象征稳健保守的理财之道。不贪大利、不冒大险，脚踏实地积累财富。“裒多益寡”也提醒我们适当回馈社会，财富才能长久。【健康】谦卦对应脾胃（坤土）和骨骼关节（艮山），提醒保持饮食节制、适度运动。心态谦和平静，有助于身心健康。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Modesty advises maintaining a humble, low-key approach as the safest workplace strategy. True competence doesn't need self-promotion—real ability will naturally be recognized. Leaders especially should win respect through humility, not authority.【Love】In relationships, Modesty represents sincerity and respect. Set aside ego, listen carefully to your partner's needs, and don't compete for dominance. A humble attitude resolves most conflicts.【Wealth】Modesty symbolizes conservative, steady financial management. Don't chase excessive profits or take unnecessary risks. 'Reducing surplus to supplement deficiency' also reminds us to give back to society.【Health】Modesty corresponds to the spleen/stomach (Kun earth) and bones/joints (Gen mountain). Maintain dietary moderation and moderate exercise. A humble, peaceful mindset promotes overall wellbeing.
historicalStoryZh: 谦卦最著名的典故是周公“一沐三捉发，一饭三吐哺”的故事。周公旦辅佐成王治理天下，位极人臣，却始终保持谦逊。每次洗头时听说有贤人来访，就三次握住湿发出去接见；每次吃饭时听说有人求见，就三次吐出口中食物去迎接。曹操在《短歌行》中引用此典：“周公吐哺，天下归心”，正是谦卦精神的最佳写照。另一个典故是孔子问礼于老子，孔子已是天下闻名的大学者，却仍虚心向老子请教，体现了“谦，德之柄也”的精神。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story of Modesty involves the Duke of Zhou, who 'three times wrung out his hair during one bath and three times spat out his food during one meal' to receive visitors. Despite holding the highest position below the king, he remained unfailingly humble. Cao Cao quoted this in his famous poem: 'The Duke of Zhou spat out his food, and all hearts turned to him.' Another story involves Confucius humbly seeking instruction from Laozi—despite being the most renowned scholar of his time, Confucius still approached Laozi as a student, perfectly embodying the principle that 'modesty is the handle of virtue.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 谦卦的错卦为履卦（第10卦），一谦一履，谦虚与践行相对应；综卦为豫卦（第16卦），谦逊与欢乐相承接——谦虚之后自然带来喜悦。互卦为解卦（第40卦），内含化解困难的力量。

judgmentEn: Enthusiasm. It furthers one to install helpers and to set armies marching.
imageZh: 雷出地奋，豫；先王以作乐崇德，殷荐之上帝，以配祖考。
imageEn: Thunder comes resounding out of the earth: the image of Enthusiasm. Thus the ancient kings made music in order to honor merit, and offered it with splendor to the Supreme Deity, inviting their ancestors to be present.
interpretationZh: 豫卦象征欢乐愉悦，雷从地中奋起，万物振奋。顺势而动，利于建功立业。
interpretationEn: Yu symbolizes enthusiasm and joyful harmony. Thunder bursts forth from the earth, stirring all things. Moving in accord with the time favors establishing great works.

lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Enthusiasm that expresses itself brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 得意忘形地宣扬欢乐，凶险。过度张扬招致灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Boasting of one's enthusiasm brings misfortune. Excessive display invites disaster.
lines.detailZh: 初六为豫卦最下一爻，阴柔居初位，与九四正应。然而初六"鸣豫"——高调宣扬自己的欢乐和得意，这是因为它与九四有应援关系，便恃宠而骄、得意忘形。"凶"字警告：真正的欢乐应当内敛含蓄，过度张扬只会招来嫉妒和灾祸。历史上许多宠臣因恃宠而骄、张扬跋扈而最终身败名裂，正是初六的写照。
lines.detailEn: The first six is the lowest line of Yu, a yin line in the initial position with a direct correspondence to the yang nine in the fourth place. Yet the first six 'boasts of enthusiasm'—loudly proclaiming its joy and self-satisfaction. This occurs because its connection to the powerful fourth line breeds arrogance and complacency. The word 'misfortune' warns that true joy should be restrained and modest; excessive display only invites jealousy and disaster. Throughout history, many favored ministers who grew arrogant through royal patronage and flaunted their status ultimately met disgrace—a perfect illustration of the first line's lesson.

lines.textZh: 六二：介于石，不终日，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Firm as a rock. Not a whole day. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚如磐石，不等一天就能明辨是非，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm as a rock. One discerns the truth before the day is over. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六二居下卦中位，得中得正，是豫卦中最吉利的一爻。"介于石"形容其品格坚如磐石，不为外界的欢乐喧嚣所动摇。"不终日"表示不等一天就能明辨吉凶——这种敏锐的判断力来自内心的坚定。在众人沉浸于欢乐时，六二能保持清醒，及时察觉危险信号。程颐评价此爻："知几其神乎"，能在事物萌芽阶段就洞察其发展方向，这是最高级的智慧。
lines.detailEn: The second six occupies the central position of the lower trigram, achieving both centrality and correctness—making it the most auspicious line in Yu. 'Firm as a rock' describes a character as solid as stone, unmoved by the surrounding revelry and noise. 'Not a whole day' means discerning fortune from misfortune before the day is out—this acute judgment springs from inner steadfastness. While others are immersed in celebration, the second six maintains clarity and detects danger signals promptly. Cheng Yi commented on this line: 'To know the incipient is divine'—the ability to perceive the direction of events at their very inception represents the highest form of wisdom.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Enthusiasm that looks upward creates remorse. Hesitation brings remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 仰望他人而生欢乐，会有悔恨。犹豫不决也会后悔。
lines.interpretationEn: Looking upward for enthusiasm creates remorse. Hesitation also brings remorse.
lines.detailZh: 六三居下卦之上，不中不正，位置尴尬。"盱豫"是仰望他人（九四）而生的欢乐——自己没有实力，只能依附强者获得快感。这种依附性的欢乐注定带来悔恨。"迟有悔"进一步警告：如果犹豫不决、不及时改正这种依附心态，悔恨会更深。六三的教训是：真正的快乐必须建立在自身实力和独立人格之上，仰人鼻息的欢乐终究是虚幻的。
lines.detailEn: The third six sits atop the lower trigram, achieving neither centrality nor correctness—an awkward position. 'Looking upward for enthusiasm' describes joy derived from gazing up at others (the fourth nine)—lacking one's own strength, one can only obtain pleasure through attachment to the powerful. This dependent joy inevitably brings remorse. 'Hesitation brings remorse' further warns that failing to promptly correct this dependent mentality deepens regret. The lesson of the third six is clear: genuine happiness must be built upon one's own capability and independent character. Joy that depends on others' favor is ultimately illusory.

lines.textZh: 九四：由豫，大有得。勿疑，朋盍簪。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: The source of enthusiasm. He achieves great things. Doubt not. You gather friends around you as a hair clasp gathers the hair.
lines.interpretationZh: 作为欢乐的源泉，大有所得。不要疑虑，朋友如簪聚发般汇聚而来。
lines.interpretationEn: The source of enthusiasm achieves great things. Have no doubt. Friends gather around you like hair held by a clasp.
lines.detailZh: 九四是豫卦唯一的阳爻，居上卦之初，是全卦欢乐的源泉和核心。"由豫"意为欢乐由此而生——九四以阳刚之德居于群阴之中，如同太阳照耀大地，自然吸引万物归附。"大有得"表示收获巨大，"勿疑"告诫不要自我怀疑。"朋盍簪"用发簪聚拢头发的意象，形容朋友和追随者自然汇聚。九四的领袖魅力不是靠权术，而是靠真诚和实力，这才是"众望所归"的真谛。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine is the sole yang line in Yu, positioned at the base of the upper trigram—the source and core of all enthusiasm in the hexagram. 'The source of enthusiasm' means joy originates from here. With yang strength amid surrounding yin lines, the fourth nine is like the sun illuminating the earth, naturally attracting all things. 'Achieves great things' indicates enormous gains, while 'doubt not' counsels against self-doubt. 'Friends gather like hair held by a clasp' uses the image of a hairpin gathering hair to describe how friends and followers naturally congregate. The fourth nine's leadership charisma comes not from manipulation but from sincerity and genuine capability—this is the true meaning of commanding universal support.

lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Persistently ill, and still does not die.
lines.interpretationZh: 长期处于困境如同久病，但始终不会灭亡。
lines.interpretationEn: Persistently ill yet never dying. Enduring hardship without succumbing.
lines.detailZh: 六五以阴柔居君位，上有九四阳刚之臣，形成"阴乘阳"的格局——君弱臣强。"贞疾"形容长期处于被压制的困境，如同慢性疾病缠身。"恒不死"则表示虽然处境艰难，但不会灭亡。这是因为六五居中位，有中庸之德护身。六五的处境像是一个能力平庸但位居高位的领导者，虽然被强势下属架空，但只要守住中道、不妄动，就能维持局面不至于崩溃。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six, a yin line in the ruler's position, sits above the strong yang fourth nine, creating a pattern of 'yin riding yang'—a weak ruler with a powerful minister. 'Persistently ill' describes the chronic predicament of being overshadowed, like suffering from a lingering disease. 'Never dying' indicates that despite the difficulty, destruction is averted. This is because the fifth six occupies the central position, protected by the virtue of the mean. The fifth six's situation resembles a leader of modest ability in a high position who, though effectively sidelined by a dominant subordinate, can maintain stability by adhering to the middle way and avoiding rash action.

lines.textEn: Six at the top: Deluded enthusiasm. But if after completion one changes, there is no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 沉迷于欢乐而昏昧，但若能及时醒悟改变，则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Blinded by enthusiasm. But if one changes after realizing the error, there is no blame.
lines.detailZh: 上六居豫卦之极，阴柔处最高位，象征沉迷于欢乐而不能自拔。"冥豫"是昏暗的欢乐——已经分不清现实与幻觉，完全沉溺在享乐之中。然而"成有渝，无咎"给出了转机：如果在事情已经形成之后能够醒悟、改变方向，仍然可以免于灾祸。上六的智慧在于"知错能改"——即使已经走到了沉迷的极端，只要愿意回头，就永远不晚。这与佛家"放下屠刀，立地成佛"的精神相通。
lines.detailEn: The top six occupies the extreme position of Yu, a yin line at the highest point, symbolizing complete immersion in pleasure beyond the point of self-awareness. 'Deluded enthusiasm' is joy shrouded in darkness—one can no longer distinguish reality from illusion, entirely lost in indulgence. Yet 'if after completion one changes, there is no blame' offers a turning point: if one awakens and changes course even after matters have already taken shape, disaster can still be averted. The wisdom of the top six lies in the capacity for self-correction—even having reached the extreme of indulgence, it is never too late to turn back. This resonates with the Buddhist teaching that 'laying down the butcher's knife, one immediately becomes a Buddha.'

overviewZh: 豫卦是《周易》第十六卦，由坤下震上组成，地上雷鸣，象征万物在春雷激荡中振奋欢悦。"豫"字本义为安逸、欢乐，引申为预备、预先。豫卦是继谦卦之后的自然发展——谦虚到了极致，便会获得众人的拥戴和欢乐。《序卦传》云："有大而能谦必豫，故受之以豫。"豫卦的核心智慧在于：真正的欢乐来自顺应天道、顺势而为，而非纵情享乐。九四为全卦唯一阳爻，居上卦之初，如同乐队指挥，引领五阴共鸣，这正是"众望所归"的领袖格局。
overviewEn: Yu (Enthusiasm) is the sixteenth hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Earth (Kun) below and Thunder (Zhen) above. Thunder resounding over the earth symbolizes the joyful stirring of all things in spring. The character 'Yu' originally means ease and joy, extended to mean preparation and anticipation. Yu naturally follows Qian (Modesty)—when modesty reaches its fullness, it naturally attracts support and joy. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'He who is great and modest will surely find enthusiasm, hence Yu follows.' The core wisdom of Yu lies in understanding that true joy comes from moving in harmony with the Way of Heaven, not from indulgence. The single yang line at the fourth position, at the base of the upper trigram, acts like a conductor leading an orchestra—five yin lines resonate in harmony, creating the image of a leader who commands universal support.
judgmentDetailZh: 豫卦卦辞"利建侯行师"简洁有力，揭示了欢乐的正确用途。"利建侯"指适宜分封诸侯、建立组织架构，因为在众人欢悦、人心归附的时刻，正是建立制度、凝聚团队的最佳时机。"行师"指适宜出兵征伐，因为士气高昂、上下同心时，军队战斗力最强。这两件事的共同点是：都需要众人的热情支持和协同配合。豫卦告诫我们，欢乐不是目的，而是手段——要善用人心振奋的时机来成就大事。正如古代明君在万民欢庆时颁布政令、整军出征，把欢乐转化为建设性的力量。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Yu—'It furthers one to install helpers and to set armies marching'—is remarkably concise yet profound. 'Installing helpers' refers to establishing feudal lords and organizational structures, because the moment when people are joyful and hearts are united is the optimal time to build institutions and consolidate teams. 'Setting armies marching' refers to military campaigns, because morale is highest and unity strongest when enthusiasm pervades. The common thread is that both endeavors require the enthusiastic support and coordinated effort of many people. Yu teaches that joy is not an end in itself but a means—one must skillfully harness the momentum of collective enthusiasm to accomplish great things. Just as wise rulers of antiquity would promulgate decrees and mobilize armies during times of popular celebration, transforming joy into constructive force.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"雷出地奋，豫；先王以作乐崇德，殷荐之上帝，以配祖考"将自然现象与礼乐文明完美结合。"雷出地奋"描绘春雷破土而出的壮观景象——沉寂了一个冬天的大地，在第一声春雷中苏醒，万物为之振奋。先王效法这一天象，创制礼乐来崇尚美德。"殷荐之上帝"指以隆重的祭祀献给上天，"以配祖考"指同时祭祀祖先。音乐在古代不仅是娱乐，更是沟通天人、教化百姓的重要工具。先王通过礼乐让百姓在欢乐中接受教化，在祭祀中感受敬畏，实现了"乐而不淫"的治理境界。
imageDetailEn: The Image text—'Thunder comes resounding out of the earth: the image of Enthusiasm. Thus the ancient kings made music in order to honor merit, and offered it with splendor to the Supreme Deity, inviting their ancestors to be present'—perfectly unites natural phenomena with ritual civilization. 'Thunder resounding from the earth' depicts the magnificent scene of spring thunder breaking through the ground—the earth, silent through winter, awakens with the first thunderclap, and all things stir with vitality. The ancient kings emulated this celestial pattern by creating ritual music to honor virtue. 'Offered with splendor to the Supreme Deity' refers to grand sacrificial offerings to Heaven, while 'inviting ancestors to be present' means simultaneously honoring ancestral spirits. In antiquity, music was far more than entertainment—it was a vital instrument for communicating between Heaven and humanity and for educating the people. Through ritual music, the ancient kings enabled the people to receive moral cultivation through joy and to experience reverence through sacrifice, achieving the ideal governance of 'joy without excess.'
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】豫卦提醒领导者要善于营造积极氛围，在团队士气高涨时推进重大项目。"利建侯"启示要在人心齐聚时建立组织架构、明确分工。但要警惕初六"鸣豫"的教训——过度庆祝和张扬会招致失败。【感情】豫卦象征感情中的甜蜜期和热恋阶段。六二"介于石"提醒即使在最欢乐的时刻也要保持清醒判断，不被感情冲昏头脑。九四"由豫"则表示真正有魅力的人自然吸引他人。【财运】顺势而为是豫卦的财运核心。在市场情绪高涨时果断出手，但要像六二一样"不终日"——快速决断，不贪恋。上六"冥豫"警告沉迷于投机快感终将失败。【健康】雷出地奋象征生命力的迸发，适合在春季开始新的健身计划。但六五"贞疾恒不死"提醒慢性病患者要有耐心，持续调养终能好转。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Yu reminds leaders to skillfully cultivate positive energy and advance major projects when team morale is high. 'Installing helpers' suggests building organizational structures and clarifying roles when hearts are united. However, heed the warning of the first line's 'boasting enthusiasm'—excessive celebration and ostentation invite failure. In relationships, Yu symbolizes the honeymoon phase and passionate romance. The second line's 'firm as a rock' reminds us to maintain clear judgment even in the most joyful moments, not letting emotions cloud reason. The fourth line's 'source of enthusiasm' indicates that truly charismatic people naturally attract others. In finance, moving with momentum is Yu's core financial wisdom. Act decisively when market sentiment is bullish, but like the second line, 'not a whole day'—make quick decisions without lingering in greed. The top line's 'deluded enthusiasm' warns that addiction to speculative thrills ultimately leads to ruin. In health, thunder bursting from the earth symbolizes the eruption of vitality, making it ideal to begin new fitness regimens in spring. The fifth line's 'persistently ill yet never dying' reminds chronic illness sufferers to be patient—sustained care will eventually bring improvement.
historicalStoryZh: 豫卦最著名的典故是周武王在牧野之战前的誓师大会。武王率军抵达牧野，面对商纣王的大军，他并未急于进攻，而是先举行隆重的祭天仪式和军前誓师，用慷慨激昂的《牧誓》激发将士斗志。全军上下群情振奋、同仇敌忾，这正是"利建侯行师"的完美诠释。另一个典故是舜帝制作《韶乐》。舜以德治天下，创制了被孔子赞为"尽善尽美"的《韶乐》，用音乐教化百姓、感通天地。据说演奏《韶乐》时百鸟来朝、凤凰起舞，这正是"先王以作乐崇德"的历史原型。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Yu is King Wu of Zhou's oath ceremony before the Battle of Muye. When King Wu led his army to Muye to face the forces of the tyrant King Zhou of Shang, he did not rush into battle. Instead, he first conducted a grand sacrificial ceremony to Heaven and delivered the stirring 'Oath of Mu' (Mu Shi) before the assembled troops, igniting their fighting spirit with passionate rhetoric. The entire army was filled with righteous enthusiasm and unified resolve—a perfect embodiment of 'installing helpers and setting armies marching.' Another famous story involves Emperor Shun's creation of the Shao Music. Shun governed through virtue and created the Shao Music, which Confucius praised as 'perfectly beautiful and perfectly good.' This music was used to educate the people and harmonize Heaven and Earth. Legend holds that when the Shao Music was performed, hundreds of birds gathered in tribute and phoenixes danced—the historical archetype of 'the ancient kings made music to honor merit.'
relatedHexagramsNote: 豫卦的错卦为小过卦（第62卦），象征由大乐转为小心谨慎；综卦为谦卦（第15卦），豫谦相序，谦虚则生欢乐；互卦为蹇卦（第39卦），提醒欢乐之中潜藏困难。
